Make sense. What we're actually envisioning here is not just another dashboarding tool,

intelligent AI agents can continuously monitor workflows, generate insights and
proactively assist teams. That's interesting. Can you give an example? Sure.
We can take an example of the reporting agent. So, an agent, AI agent can automatically collect

reports every morning. Again, we can take an example of the inventory agent,
like another AI agent that continuously monitor inventory patterns, and alert team before
stocks to a shortage happens. That would actually save a huge amount of
operational effort for us. What kind of text tech would support something like this?
Okay. So, for the AI agent architecture, we would suggest for the front-end,
we can use the ReactJS TypeScript tailwind Cases. For the backend, we can use Python,

And for database, basis, we can use PostgreSQL or Redis. And for the infrastructure,
we can use AWS or Docker or Kubernetes. This setup allows us to build scalable AI agents
that can interact with business systems very securely. And how long would the development take?
Okay. So, we would recommend a phased layout rollout maybe. Like we have the timelines
for every phase. Like for the discovery and AI agent design, I think it will take around
two weeks. Then for the core platform development, I think it will take six to eight weeks maybe.
